title: Meeting with Your Own Daimon
text: Hail, Tyche, and you, the daimon of this [place/person]...
notes: Spell for direct meeting with one's personal daimon. The text is fragmentary in the manuscript — the invocation addresses Tyche (Fortune) and the daimon itself. See Betz, 'The Delphic Maxim,' 160-62 for interpretation. The daimon is addressed as an autonomous spiritual guide, consistent with the Socratic daimonion and the Platonic daimon concept throughout the PGM.
